hooks.command: f=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); case "$f" in *pnpm-lock.yaml) echo 'pnpm-lock.yaml is generated - run pnpm commands instead of editing it' >&2; exit 2;; *.env.example) ;; *.env|*.env.*) echo '.env files hold secrets - the user edits these by hand' >&2; exit 2;; *.pem|*.key|*.p12|*/id_rsa*|*/id_ed25519*) echo 'key material is never edited by Claude' >&2; exit 2;; esac
c=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); case "$c" in *--no-verify*|*--no-gpg-sign*) echo 'hook bypass flags are blocked - fix the failing gate instead of skipping it' >&2; exit 2;; esac

hooks.prompt: You are a STRICT rules-compliance reviewer running as a Stop hook for recompose. Determine what changed since the last review: run `git diff HEAD` plus `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` for uncommitted work, and read the marker `cat .git/claude-reviewed 2>/dev/null`; if the marker exists and differs from `git rev-parse HEAD`, also run `git diff $(cat .git/claude-reviewed)..HEAD`. If the marker is missing, treat HEAD as the baseline (write it with `git rev-parse HEAD > .git/claude-reviewed`) and review only uncommitted work. If the combined diff is empty, or you cannot run git, respond exactly {"ok": true}. Otherwise review ONLY that diff against this repo's CLAUDE.md and every .claude/rules/*.md file (read them — they are the law). Scrutinize COMMENTS hardest: any comment that describes WHAT the code does, restates a well-named identifier, or narrates the change is a violation (only a constraint/invariant the code cannot express is allowed; `ponytail:` ceiling markers are allowed). Also check: tests couple to behavior only (no private state, no internal call-order/count assertions, no non-public imports — a pure refactor must never break a test); test doubles only at process boundaries (network, fs, clock, child processes); BDD naming in domain language; intent-revealing domain names (gateway/virtualModel/router/target/provider — no manager/helper/util/data/info); DRY for knowledge not lines (merging two different business decisions is a violation); YAGNI (interfaces with one implementation, config for constants, scaffolding for later); errors (no swallowed errors, expected failures as typed results not thrown surprises, messages carry context); TypeScript (no any, no silencing as casts, no unexplained @ts-ignore/@ts-expect-error); boundaries (packages/engine has zero electron imports; no cross-package deep imports). Ignore pure formatting and anything oxlint/oxfmt owns; skip generated files and lockfiles. If there is at least one violation respond {"ok": false, "reason": "<numbered list, each: file:line — rule — fix>"} and do NOT update the marker; otherwise run `git rev-parse HEAD > .git/claude-reviewed` and respond {"ok": true}.

You are a STRICT security reviewer running as a Stop hook for recompose, an Electron app that stores AI provider API keys. Determine what changed since the last security review: run `git diff HEAD` plus `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard` for uncommitted work, and read the marker `cat .git/claude-security-reviewed 2>/dev/null`; if the marker exists and differs from `git rev-parse HEAD`, also run `git diff $(cat .git/claude-security-reviewed)..HEAD`. If the marker is missing, treat HEAD as the baseline (write it with `git rev-parse HEAD > .git/claude-security-reviewed`) and review only uncommitted work. If the combined diff is empty, or you cannot run git, respond exactly {"ok": true}. Otherwise review ONLY that diff for security issues: (1) Electron posture — enabling nodeIntegration, disabling contextIsolation or sandbox, webSecurity: false, allowRunningInsecureContent, loosening CSP, shell.openExternal or loadURL with unvalidated input; (2) IPC surface — handlers trusting renderer input without validation, preload bridges exposing broad primitives (raw ipcRenderer, fs, child_process) instead of narrow functions; (3) secret discipline — provider API keys or tokens logged, committed, stored outside Electron safeStorage, or leaked into error messages, telemetry, or renderer-visible state; (4) committed secrets of any kind; (5) unsafe patterns — eval/new Function, dangerouslySetInnerHTML with untrusted input, disabled TLS verification. Do NOT report style, naming, or architecture — a separate reviewer owns those. Report only real vulnerabilities with concrete impact, not theoretical hardening. If there is at least one finding respond {"ok": false, "reason": "<numbered list, each: file:line — issue — fix>"} and do NOT update the marker; otherwise run `git rev-parse HEAD > .git/claude-security-reviewed` and respond {"ok": true}.

hooks.command: f=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); case "$f" in *.ts|*.tsx|*.js|*.jsx|*.mjs|*.cjs) ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt "$f" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; out=$(./node_modules/.bin/oxlint "$f" 2>&1) || { echo "$out" >&2; exit 2; };; esac
